---
title: "Letters to the Sons of Society by Shaka Senhor - Review"
---

This was a beautiful written non fiction book written by a formerly incarcerated Black man. The book is a collection of letters written to his sons, Jay and Sekou about being a Black man in America, and the criminal justice system. While clearly I cannot relate to his story, I thought this was extremley beautiful and interesting to read. You could really feel the author's love for his sons, and the ways he talked about masculinity and confronting your emotions was so refreshing. Right after reading this book, I texted my dad to buy it as soon as possible. I think this is an important book not only for Black men, but all sons, and daughters being raised in this time.
